title: Venue Profile
description: #394 scholar-declared venue profile (spec docs/design/2026-06-10-394-submission-package-verifier-spec.md §4). The Family B comparison source for scripts/verify_submission_package.py --venue-profile. STANDALONE schema — never embedded in the passport entry schema (the Invariant 11 pattern). Declared values ONLY: the profile is never scraped or inferred from the journal name (the R-L3-2-D no-inference mirror) — a check whose field is null/absent reports NOT-CHECKED, never a guessed limit. Collected (optionally) at intake Step 3 when a target journal is named; storable and re-feedable across runs like any declared input.

venue_name.description: Display only — NEVER used for inference or lookup (R-L3-2-D mirror).

word_limit.description: Manuscript word ceiling (B1). Compared with a ±2% tolerance for format-conversion noise (spec §3.2).

word_count_scope.description: What the venue's counting rules include. The report declares what was actually counted; null = the verifier counts body_only and says so (the method is declared, never silently assumed venue-exact — spec §3.2/§10 item 4).

abstract_word_limit.description: Abstract word ceiling (B2). Same ±2% tolerance class as word_limit.

keyword_range.description: Accepted keyword count range, inclusive (B3). Exact comparison — no tolerance.

required_sections.description: Section names the venue requires (B4), e.g. ["Data Availability", "CRediT"]. Matched case-insensitively against manuscript headings (declared name contained in a heading).

acknowledgments_forbidden_in_blind.description: Scholar-declared: the venue requires acknowledgments to be removed from the blind-review version. ONLY when true does A4 (acknowledgments present in the anonymized variant) become strict-eligible — by default A4's deterministic signal stays advisory because the de-anonymization judgment is the scholar's (spec §3.1 load-bearing).

blind_review.description: Review model. `double` triggers the Family A residue scan even when no anonymized variant is present (slice 3, spec §3.1) — a declared-double-blind package with no blind version is itself a fail.

declared_by.description: The only provenance value that exists (spec §4): the profile is scholar-declared, full stop. The CLI refuses a profile without this stamp rather than consuming values of unknown origin.
